narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school only to have your application turned away because a model flagged a 55.5% chance you might not pass the bar exam feels deeply unfair, and I'm here to walk you through why it landed there, like sharing the story behind a tough but honest conversation. Picture the model piecing together your journey: it zeroed in first on your Year 1 grades, placing you in the bottom decile at school, which stood out as the strongest signal pulling toward struggle ahead, almost like an early stumble that set a challenging pace. Then came your LSAT score of 38, the next big piece, whispering that competing in the high-stakes world of law might be an uphill battle from the start. Your Year 3 decile in the 1st rank reinforced that pattern, suggesting the initial hurdles didn't fully turn around. Layered on top was your undergraduate GPA of 2.9, which didn't shine brightly enough to counter the trend. Even factors like being a white male, coming from a high family income bracket, and planning full-time studies nudged the picture toward risk, as the model saw them fitting a profile where others in similar spots have faltered before. It wasn't one thing alone, but this mosaic of your path—those low deciles and scores blending into a cautious forecast—that tipped the scales to predict failure more likely than not. This isn't about your worth or potential; it's the model reading patterns from countless stories like yours. You've got high family support and full-time focus in your corner—those could be levers to pull as you strengthen your academics. Keep pushing; law school doors might open wider with a bit more polish on those early grades and test scores. You've already shown drive by aiming for this; channel it forward. We're rooting for your next chapter.
